summary: A 1947 Washington Post clipping reports that a Catholic priest in Grafton, Wisconsin heard a swishing and whirring noise followed by a thud and mild explosion, then found a round metal disc about 18 inches in diameter and one-eighth inch thick in his yard, which he turned over to the FBI. The article notes the disc was warm, weighed four to five pounds, had a hole with wires and gadgets inside, and situates the discovery amid other nationwide reports of flying discs.
